context_label: exceptional_bottleneck (scarce dispatchable/clean baseload for AI) / regulated-ish merchant-utility hybrid

module_signals.finding: Live data refreshed to 2026-06-22 with primary sources. Q1 2026 10-Q now confirms net debt ~$21.3B (LT $16,994M + ST $5,102M - cash $800M) and 361.19M shares (cover, Apr 30 2026) -- RESOLVES the seed's disputed C011 leverage estimate. Calpine consideration $21,835M (10-Q). FY26 guide $11-12 affirmed at Q1. Remaining gaps: MSFT/Crane PPA price still officially undisclosed (secondary estimates $100-115/MWh, unconfirmed); year-by-year hedge ratios undisclosed. Freshness gate PASS (3 price sources within 0.21%).

CEG owns the physical scarce input -- ~32 GW carbon-free nuclear baseload at ~95% capacity factor, near-zero marginal fuel, effectively un-buildable (no new large US reactor on time/budget in a generation; 10-15yr greenfield). The AI-power demand leg is accelerating: hyperscaler 2026 capex ~$725B (+77%). The bottleneck is owned, not merely depended-on -- the key contrast with NBIS.

Durable scarcity rent: ~95% CF nuclear at ~$0 fuel sells into PJM at gas-set clearing prices; the new lever is long-dated PTC-floored hyperscaler PPAs (Crane->MSFT 20yr, Meta->Clinton 20yr). No capex treadmill (fleet exists; Crane is a $1.6B DOE-backed restart). Operator (Dominguez) runs ~95% CF, beats adj-EPS repeatedly, clean governance (no super-voting), per-share oriented ($5B buyback + >=10% dividend growth). Durability ceiling: merchant-power cyclicality + Calpine gas dilution (carbon-free 90%->58%) cap this below Core per METHODOLOGY.

Positive, growing owner earnings (FY25 adj EPS $9.39, FCF ~$1.3B standalone) -- unlike NBIS's negative OE. Clean accounting; the dilution was a discrete, accretion-justified Calpine equity issuance, not chronic SBC. KEY REFRESH: post-Calpine leverage is LESS alarming than the seed feared -- primary 10-Q net debt ~$21.3B and management pro-forma leverage ~2.25x (forward-EBITDA), with a $3.4B debt-reduction plan to 2027 toward ~2.0x. LS Power $5B/4.4GW divestiture COMPLETED (cash in). Q1'26 +64% revenue is an acquisition artifact, not organic. Caveat: combined-entity FCF and year-by-year hedge ratios still not cleanly reported.

Of 5 pre-registered kills: (a) FERC blocks co-location DEFUSED (5-0 enabling order; front-of-meter flagship); (b) power->PTC floor LIVE but FLOORED (~$25/MWh to 2032; 2027 open ~$34); (c) Calpine dilutive/over-levered PARTIAL but DE-RISKED vs seed (leverage now ~2.25x pro-forma not 3.8x; LS Power cash in); (d) PTC repealed NOT TRIGGERED (survived OBBBA); (e) shared switch ACCELERATING +77%. The live failure mode is narrowed to the capped flagship (Crane upside sold to MSFT) + still-no-NEW-premium-nuclear-PPA -- the 'quality-at-fair-price trap' where equity is dead money at ~24x absent a new catalyst. SINGLE-FACTOR TIE: CEG rides the SAME AI-power buildout as NBIS/VRT -- stacking them doubles one factor, not diversifies (METHODOLOGY sec5).

At $277.64: mkt cap ~$100B, fwd P/E ~24x, EV/EBITDA ~15x. Premium to merchant peers (VST ~16x, TLN ~17-18x, NRG ~18x), compressed from >35x at the $413 high (-33%). Street targets $296-441, median ~$360 (23 analysts, Buy) -- even the LOW target sits above the current price. Asymmetry: bear ~$210-230 (-20%, floored by PTC), base ~$300-340 (+10-22%), bull ~$400+ (+45%). Margin of safety EXISTS (downside is multiple-compression on an intact, PTC-floored franchise, not earnings collapse) -- so M6 does NOT price-cap below completeness. STARTER now; add toward $230-250 or on a confirmed NEW premium PPA + clean deleverage.

starter_zone: $250-300 (current $277.64 sits here -- fair-not-cheap, margin of safety vs own froth + vs Street)
trim_or_no_chase_zone: >=$370 (Street median; reassess vs froth) / >=$414 (>30x, froth re-emerges -> trim)
binding_constraint: Info-completeness ~76/100 caps the verdict at STARTER (one parallel refresh pass = DECISION_DRAFT, not COMPLETE). The economic binding constraint is the capped flagship + absence of a confirmed NEW premium-nuclear hyperscaler PPA: Crane's upside is contracted to Microsoft at an undisclosed (likely fixed) price, so the equity re-rate hinges on the next deal, not on the moat existing. Durability ceiling (merchant cyclicality + Calpine gas dilution) independently caps below CORE.
kill_criteria: Calpine deleverage off-track: pro-forma ~2.25x / target ~2.0x by 2027 stalls, a further DOJ/FERC divestiture slips, or a 10-Q shows leverage rising rather than falling -> TRIM

Capped flagship + extended PPA drought: no NEW (post-Meta) premium-nuclear hyperscaler PPA signs AND open-year power curves soften toward the ~$25/MWh PTC floor -> equity is dead money at ~24x (gates the UPGRADE; absence is the binding constraint)
FERC final PJM co-location definition lands hostile in a way that reaches front-of-meter PPAs (low probability after the 5-0 order; CEG flagship insulated) -> re-open kill (a)
Hyperscaler 2027 capex guide DECELERATES (shared-switch demand leg, common to NBIS/VRT/CEG) -> DOWNGRADE the whole AI-power cohort
Open-year nuclear volume priced at/near the ~$25/MWh PTC floor for multiple years -> upside compression (downside still floored; not a permanent-impairment kill)
runner_dissent: Mechanically the live refresh STRENGTHENS the seed thesis -- the scariest balance-sheet fear (post-Calpine leverage 3.8x) is overstated: the primary Q1'26 10-Q shows net debt ~$21.3B and management runs pro-forma leverage at ~2.25x with a completed $5B LS Power sale, so kill (c) is more de-risked than the seed's 'PARTIAL TRIGGER' implied. That argues the mid-20s multiple is better supported than at 2026-06-19. BUT three honest cautions hold the verdict at STARTER, not higher: (1) at ~$278 / ~24x there is real but THIN margin of safety -- the -33% de-rate cut froth (>35x), not absolute value; ~24x still embeds a 6-8 turn premium over 16-18x merchant peers, so a Calpine-gas-driven re-rating toward a merchant multiple is a live downside even with the PTC floor. (2) The single biggest upside swing -- the NEXT PPA price -- remains unseen; Crane's economics are locked to an undisclosed MSFT price (secondary $100-115/MWh estimates are unconfirmed and must NOT enter facts), and the one genuinely-new deal found (CyrusOne 380 MW) is Calpine GAS, not a premium-nuclear re-rate catalyst. (3) This is one parallel pass on a seed -- DECISION_DRAFT, info ~76, NOT a COMPLETE multi-pass; the SEC 10-K/10-Q headline figures and combined-entity FCF still warrant a second primary confirmation before any CORE upgrade. CONCENTRATION FLAG: for a holder already long NBIS + GOOGL + BTC, adding CEG doubles the AI-capex/liquidity factor rather than diversifying -- size accordingly.

sources_used.url_or_path: NRC draft EA/FONSI, Crane restart (Federal Register)
